questions.question: The  Chinese  government  is  concerned  about  the  level  of  poverty  and  the  need  for  more resources in low-income regions of the country. As a result, it is increasing its expenditure in those regions and reducing it in high-income regions. Which economic concept does this government policy illustrate? A diseconomies of scale, B market forces, C opportunity cost, D specialisation
questions.text: The  Chinese  government  is  concerned  about  the  level  of  poverty  and  the  need  for  more resources in low-income regions of the country. As a result, it is increasing its expenditure in those regions and reducing it in high-income regions. Which economic concept does this government policy illustrate? A diseconomies of scale, B market forces, C opportunity cost, D specialisation

questions.question: An airline needs people and equipment. These can be divided into factors of production. What is allocated to its correct factor of production? people or equipment    factor of production A aircraft capital, B airline owners labour, C computer network land, D pilots enterprise
questions.text: An airline needs people and equipment. These can be divided into factors of production. What is allocated to its correct factor of production? people or equipment    factor of production A aircraft capital, B airline owners labour, C computer network land, D pilots enterprise

questions.question: The diagram shows a production possibility curve (PPC) for country Z. Government policy is to move the economy from point X to point Y on the PPC. capital goods O consumer goods X Y What is the most likely effect of this policy? A Living standards rise in the short run., B More capital goods are produced., C Prices of consumer goods increase., D Total output increases. 3
questions.text: The diagram shows a production possibility curve (PPC) for country Z. Government policy is to move the economy from point X to point Y on the PPC. capital goods O consumer goods X Y What is the most likely effect of this policy? A Living standards rise in the short run., B More capital goods are produced., C Prices of consumer goods increase., D Total output increases. 3
options: A Living standards rise in the short run.
B More capital goods are produced.
C Prices of consumer goods increase.

questions.question: The table shows examples of changes in an economy. Which combination has two microeconomic changes? change 1 change 2 A an increase in the rate of inflation a rise in the average earnings of healthcare professionals B an increase in the rate of inflation a rise in the rate of unemployment C a reduction in output in the car industry a rise in the average earnings of healthcare professionals D a reduction in output in the car industry a rise in the rate of unemployment

questions.question: The diagram shows the supply of motor vehicles. O price quantity S 1 S 2 What will cause a shift in the supply curve from S 1 to S 2 ? A a decrease in the profits of suppliers, B a fall in the real incomes of customers, C a more efficient assembly line using robots, D a rise in the cost of motor vehicle components 4
questions.text: The diagram shows the supply of motor vehicles. O price quantity S 1 S 2 What will cause a shift in the supply curve from S 1 to S 2 ? options: A a decrease in the profits of suppliers
B a fall in the real incomes of customers
C a more efficient assembly line using robots
D a rise in the cost of motor vehicle components 4

questions.question: The diagram shows the demand for and supply of plastic bags. The original equilibrium price is P. price D 3 S 3 S 1 S 2 D 1 D 2 P Q quantity O How would the introduction of a unit tax on plastic bags be shown? A Demand would shift to D 2 ., B Demand would shift to D 3 ., C Supply would shift to S 2 ., D Supply would shift to S 3 .

questions.question: What is the significance to firms of price elasticity of demand? A It allows firms to measure the effect of a change in the price of their competitors., B It allows firms to measure their ability to change production due to a change in price., C It allows firms to predict the change in costs of production., D It allows firms to predict the effect of a change in price on their total revenue.
questions.text: What is the significance to firms of price elasticity of demand? A It allows firms to measure the effect of a change in the price of their competitors., B It allows firms to measure their ability to change production due to a change in price., C It allows firms to predict the change in costs of production., D It allows firms to predict the effect of a change in price on their total revenue.
options: A It allows firms to measure the effect of a change in the price of their competitors.
B It allows firms to measure their ability to change production due to a change in price.
C It allows firms to predict the change in costs of production.
D It allows firms to predict the effect of a change in price on their total revenue.

questions.question: In a free market, market failure causes a misallocation of resources. What is not an example of market failure? A external costs of production, B internal diseconomies of scale, C over-consumption of demerit goods, D under-consumption of merit goods 5

questions.question: Which advantage do coins have that makes them more suitable than paper banknotes to act as money? A They are more divisible., B They are more durable., C They are more portable., D They are more recognisable.
questions.text: Which advantage do coins have that makes them more suitable than paper banknotes to act as money? A They are more divisible., B They are more durable., C They are more portable., D They are more recognisable.

questions.question: Which statement about different income groups is correct? A High-income groups do not need to borrow money., B High-income groups save less money than low-income groups., C Low-income groups find it easier to borrow than high-income groups., D Low-income groups save a smaller percentage of their income than high-income groups.
questions.text: Which statement about different income groups is correct? A High-income groups do not need to borrow money., B High-income groups save less money than low-income groups., C Low-income groups find it easier to borrow than high-income groups., D Low-income groups save a smaller percentage of their income than high-income groups.
options: A High-income groups do not need to borrow money.
B High-income groups save less money than low-income groups.
C Low-income groups find it easier to borrow than high-income groups.
D Low-income groups save a smaller percentage of their income than high-income groups.

questions.question: A musician has a choice of playing for an orchestra in either Germany or England. Which  combination  of  incomes  and  cost  of  living  is most  likely  to  cause  her  to  choose  the German orchestra? incomes and cost of living in Germany compared with those in England pre-tax (gross) income   after-tax (net) income cost of living A higher higher higher, B higher lower lower, C lower higher higher, D lower higher lower 6

questions.question: A firm produces bicycles. It uses a combination of workers and machines. The table shows the output from different combinations of workers and machines. Which combination shows the greatest labour productivity? workers   machines    output A 4 5 17, B 8 5 32, C 10 5 45, D 20 5 76

questions.question: Two large-scale firms involved in chemical manufacturing wish to merge. Why might the government decide to prevent the merger? A It enables greater economies of scale to operate., B It increases productivity per worker employed., C It reduces competition in the industry., D It results in higher wages paid to labour.
questions.text: Two large-scale firms involved in chemical manufacturing wish to merge. Why might the government decide to prevent the merger? A It enables greater economies of scale to operate., B It increases productivity per worker employed., C It reduces competition in the industry., D It results in higher wages paid to labour.
options: A It enables greater economies of scale to operate.
B It increases productivity per worker employed.
C It reduces competition in the industry.
D It results in higher wages paid to labour.

questions.question: Which change is an example of monetary policy? A a depreciation of the foreign exchange rate, B a reduction of unemployment benefits, C an increased import tax, D the introduction of import quotas

B a reduction of unemployment benefits
D the introduction of import quotas

questions.question: What is the most likely effect of increasing the rate of interest? A Investment increases., B The cost of government borrowing falls., C The exchange rate falls., D The rate of inflation falls.
questions.text: What is the most likely effect of increasing the rate of interest? A Investment increases., B The cost of government borrowing falls., C The exchange rate falls., D The rate of inflation falls.
options: B The cost of government borrowing falls.
D The rate of inflation falls.

questions.question: The table shows the percentage (%) contribution of agriculture to the GDP of three countries in a year. country     % X 18.5 Y 3.9 Z 2.2 What can be concluded from the table? A Agricultural production in country Z was not as productive as it was in country Y., B Country X was likely to be less developed as a country than country Z., C Country Y was likely to import more agricultural produce than country Z., D Total agricultural production was higher in country X than in country Z.

C Country Y was likely to import more agricultural produce than country Z.

questions.question: Which row shows the potential benefits of free trade to consumers, producers and the economy? consumers producers economy A higher income lower costs deflation, B lower quality higher prices greater equality, C lower prices increased competition   higher current account deficit, D more choices larger market lower unemployment

B lower quality higher prices greater equality
C lower prices increased competition   higher current account deficit
D more choices larger market lower unemployment
